source: eQTL Catalogue (github.com/eQTL-Catalogue) via human-genetics MCP connector
method: For each trio gene, tested cis-eQTL associations (p<=1e-5) across CD4/Treg/T-cell gene-expression datasets. celltype_matched_eqtl := (>=1 dataset with a significant cis-eQTL).

CD226.evidence: sig cis-eQTL in 4/11 curated CD4/Treg datasets incl OneK1K CD4_TCM (-log10p=30), BLUEPRINT T-cell (-log10p=12.2)
CD226.note: rs763361 itself is a coding missense (Gly307Ser), not the eQTL lead; the gene is eQTL-active in CD4 T-cells.

RASGRP1.evidence: ZERO significant cis-eQTLs across ALL 120 T-cell datasets; risk variant rs72727394 not tested/present in CD4 T-cells
RASGRP1.note: LOAD-BEARING veto for the money-shot: RASGRP1=WITHHELD is now confirmed against live data, not hand-typed.

PRKCQ.evidence: sig cis-eQTL in 3/11 CD4/Treg datasets — prior hand-typed value (False) was WRONG
PRKCQ.note: NON-BINDING: PRKCQ is vetoed on the GENETIC FLOOR (sub-GWS, GA=0.162), independent of its eQTL status. Correcting this boolean does NOT change PRKCQ's WITHHELD decision — which is exactly the point of a fail-closed gate.

money_shot_integrity: The believe/veto split (CD226 GO vs RASGRP1 WITHHELD) is decided by celltype_matched_eqtl, and BOTH values are now confirmed by live eQTL Catalogue queries: CD226 True (4/11), RASGRP1 False (0/120). The one boolean that was wrong (PRKCQ) is non-binding because genetics already vetoes PRKCQ.
